$comment: telemetry.v1 — FROZEN 2026-07-09. Schema for the body of POST https://admin.windyword.ai/v1/events (per-emitter bearer token). Field names match the LIVE ingest as probed in docs/PROBE_RESULTS.md and re-probed during Task 0.4 (the ingest requires service/event_type/actor_type AND ts — the ts requirement surfaced only when a ts-less event 422'd on 2026-07-09; the genome's earlier sketch's 'event' field is event_type here). CONTENT-FREE BY CONSTRUCTION: additionalProperties:false everywhere and no free-text fields — ids, enums, timestamps, and numbers only. A payload carrying 'transcript', 'message', or any other unlisted key is schema-invalid (the live ingest 422s the same way). Emitted fire-and-forget by telemetry/emit.py (ADR-WA-001): async, ≤200ms, swallows all errors, no-op unless configured. Change control per contracts/README.md. ADDITIVE v1.1 (2026-07-11): event_type 'control.action' + optional non-content fields 'ok' (boolean) and 'mode' (closed enum), required by control.mcp.v1's telemetry block ({tool, ok, error?, mode}); the frozen v1 vocabulary predates the control surface. ADDITIVE v1.2 (2026-07-12): event_type 'update.rolled_back' (the out-of-process watcher's staged-rollout tripwire — an update that failed to attest and was rolled back) + 'tool' enum widened with the 24 control.mcp.v1 tool names: control.action events were schema-INVALID here while the LIVE ingest accepted them (re-probed 2026-07-12, 202 for tool:'enter_safe_mode'); the schema now agrees with both the ingest and control.mcp.v1's telemetry block. ADDITIVE v1.3 (2026-07-15): operational note — the ≤200ms figure governs the CALLER (emit() enqueues onto a daemon thread and returns immediately); the WIRE timeout is implementation-tunable via WINDYTALK_TELEMETRY_TIMEOUT (default 1.5s). The 200ms wire ceiling silently dropped 100% of events after the AWS→VPS ingest migration (TLS handshake alone exceeds it from residential uplinks); still fire-and-forget, still lossy-by-design.
title: Windy Talk telemetry event batch

event_type.$comment: Dotted event names, not free text. The v1 vocabulary; additive growth via v1.1.

actor_type.$comment: Live ingest requires human|agent|system (NOT 'user') — verified against admin.windyword.ai during Task 1.8.

actor_id.$comment: Required by the live ingest when actor_type is human or agent (system is exempt). Pre-1.7 this is the session id; Task 1.7 sets it to the Eternitas passport / agent id.

model.$comment: Model id via Mind — an identifier, never output text.

latency_ms.$comment: The §0.1 gates, measured per voice-session.v1 §8. Numbers only.

tool.$comment: hands.mcp.v1 tool name for tool.invoked events, or (additive v1.2) the control.mcp.v1 tool name for control.action events — the name only, never args. Coverage of BOTH contracts is drift-tested (tests/test_contracts.py).

error_code.$comment: A code (e.g. voice-session.v1 §9 codes), never a message string.

region.$comment: Coarse only (country/region code) — granularity is a Grant privacy call (ADR-058 D10).

metadata.$comment: INTEL-CONTRACT-V2 non-content descriptors. session.start requires app_version+os+install_id; session.end requires install_id (enforced live by the ingest). Whitelisted keys only — content-free.

ok.$comment: control.action only: did the executed control-surface action succeed (control.mcp.v1 telemetry).

mode.$comment: control.action only: the supervisor health mode at execution (control.mcp.v1 telemetry).

allOf.$comment: Live ingest rule: actor_id is required when the actor is human or agent.
